observations.note: Turnover ratio 84.7851 is 169x over the 0.50 threshold. This is not a borderline call; firing is dispositive. No reasonable implementation choice could move the ratio below 0.50 without fundamentally changing the mechanic frequency.

All 6 metrics (closed_trades, net_pnl, expectancy, sharpe, maxdd, annual_turnover) byte-match P6 IS sealed numbers. Strong evidence of algorithm equivalence between P6 IS and P6.5 cost-stress simulator core.

S2 net_pnl ($87,464) > S1 net_pnl ($85,976) is non-monotonic (~1.73% reversal). Plausible from per-trade slippage interactions; within noise band; does not refute the broader monotonic-decreasing trend S0 -> S4 (S0 highest at $102,795 -> S4 lowest at $79,058). NOT a defect.

DA8=B + DA9=C effectively prevented DR10 branch_b (S2 cost_drag 2.35pct < 5pct) from firing, but were structurally unable to prevent branch_a (annual_turnover 84.79 >> 0.50). This corroborates T1 alignment review's honest-take observation that sizing-based DR10 mitigation is PARTIAL by design.

DR3 (zero-cost-only survival) does NOT fire; DR5 (tier flip) does NOT fire. All 5 cost tiers retain positive expectancy + net_pnl + sharpe. The candidate has a real cost-survivable edge; DR10 rejection is about turnover frequency, not edge erosion.
observations.topic: Edge survives at every cost tier

observations.note: K1/K2/K4/K9 all clear at S1; reported all 4 A-gates pass at every tier. The candidate would otherwise be ELIGIBLE_FOR_OOS at S1 if DR10 did not fire. DR10 RF1 precedence terminates the chain at IS.

All 6 predecessor anchor seals enumerated in the P6.5 commit message verify against chain history. Tier-spec, plan-lock, P2, P3 BUILD, P4 SMOKE, P6 IS, and CSV sha all consistent.

sizing_mitigation_efficacy_analysis.alignment_review_consistency: This audit corroborates the T1 alignment review (commit c6bf9ae) honest-take that 'sizing-based mitigation is PARTIAL'; the alignment review specifically called out: 'For future RSI(2)-class candidates, this is a real lesson: sizing-based DR10 mitigation does NOT prevent DR10 firing if the threshold itself is too tight for the mechanic's structural turnover.'
sizing_mitigation_efficacy_analysis.da9_c_start_cash_200k: doubles base capital vs DA9=A baseline 100k
sizing_mitigation_efficacy_analysis.load_bearing_insight: DA8+DA9 sizing levers are PARTIAL DR10 mitigation by design. They address branch_b (cost_drag) only. Branch_a (turnover) requires either: (a) DA19=B (raise turnover threshold from 0.50 to 1.00; was offered at T1 DRAFT but not selected at SEAL; parallel s13-d1 chose DA-default similar to T1); or (b) lower-frequency mechanic family (longer-period RSI, slower oscillator, or different family entirely)
sizing_mitigation_efficacy_analysis.mitigation_outcome_branch_b: S2 cost_drag 2.35pct < 5pct threshold (branch_b does NOT fire)
sizing_mitigation_efficacy_analysis.mitigation_target_branch: DR10 branch_b (s2_cost_drag); achieved by halving per-dollar cost pressure
sizing_mitigation_efficacy_analysis.why_sizing_does_not_affect_turnover_rati: Annual turnover is defined as a ratio (dollar-turnover-traded per year / portfolio-equity). Doubling capital (DA9=C: $100k -> $200k) AND halving per-trade-risk (DA8=B: 1.0pct -> 0.5pct) produces the same number of contracts-per-trade (0.5pct of $200k = 1.0pct of $100k = same dollar risk amount) AND the same dollar-turnover-per-trade. The ratio between turnover and equity is therefore invariant under this proportional scaling. Annual turnover is intrinsic to the mechanic's signal frequency (RSI(2) bi-directional generates ~34 trades/year on MNQ.c.0 daily) and per-trade position size relative to equity (0.5pct risk -> position size proportional to ATR-stop). Neither DA8 nor DA9 can move the ratio.
